currentFocus: Aurora 1.4.1 is now the active patch-release candidate for hosted `/dev` and hosted `/beta`, while hosted `/production` remains stable on the shipped 1.4.0 public family until explicit promotion.
strapline: Aurora 1.4.1 is the focused patch path for beta review: Stage 3 challenge keepers, audio/theme clarity, beta sign-in repair, and stronger release/security gates.

timeline.title: Foundation
timeline.summary: Browser build, harness, public hosting, and release identity are in place.

timeline.title: 1.0.0 Aurora Launch
timeline.summary: Aurora's first public production launch established the initial live arcade slice, shared scoring, pilot identity, replay viewing, and the first real beta-to-production release ladder.

timeline.title: 1.0.0 Post-Launch Score Submit Hotfix
timeline.summary: The first post-launch hotfix repaired shared score submission for signed-in runs and added a refresh reminder so stale hosted tabs could pick up live fixes cleanly.

timeline.title: 1.0.1 Input And Feedback Hotfix
timeline.summary: The 1.0.1 hotfix simplified alternate controls, added focus-loss input reset, and cleaned up player feedback delivery right after launch.

timeline.title: 1.0.2 Movement Regression Hotfix
timeline.summary: The 1.0.2 emergency repair fixed the live movement regression introduced after launch and added stronger smoke coverage so obvious input failures are caught earlier.

timeline.title: 1.2.0 Platinum Release 1
timeline.summary: Aurora now ships as the first playable application on Platinum, with hosted `/dev`, hosted `/beta`, and hosted `/production` established as distinct lanes.

timeline.title: Documentation Hardening
timeline.summary: The hosted docs set is now part of the release contract, with first-class project, platform, player, and release pages required for meaningful x.y releases.

timeline.title: 1.2.1 Aurora Trust Fixes
timeline.summary: The 1.2.1 patch shipped the dual-fighter survivor fix, challenge-stage bonus-stage numbering, game-over carry suppression, expanded carry-state coverage, and a clearer platform/application front-door copy boundary.

timeline.title: 1.2.2 Runtime Hardening
timeline.summary: The 1.2.2 patch shipped runtime-loop hardening, restart-time async score-submit safety, a late-run ship-loss soak gate in the release process, and Aurora-scoped configurable bonus-ship awards.

timeline.title: 1.2.3 Aurora Score-Surface Polish
timeline.summary: The 1.2.3 patch shipped clearer wait-mode score rotation, leaderboard trust context, and lightweight score filtering without changing Platinum shell ownership or broad gameplay rules.

timeline.title: 1.2.3 Beta Refresh
timeline.summary: Hosted `/beta` now runs the `f6ba6c2` candidate line with refreshed timing, movement evidence, audio correspondence, production-ready defaults, and committed quality artifacts.

timeline.title: 1.2.3 Production Refresh
timeline.summary: Hosted `/production` now runs the `f6ba6c2` release line with refreshed beta evidence, synced public project pages, and committed release-readiness documentation.

timeline.title: Multi-Machine Bootstrap And Release Control
timeline.summary: Aurora now has a one-command machine bootstrap path, a committed release-authority contract, and public-sync verification that includes the rendered root project homepage.

timeline.title: 1.2.3 Trust And Pilot-Surface Refresh
timeline.summary: The current patch line fixes signed-in end-of-run pilot locking, recent-flight and local-score visibility, a last-life runtime freeze, and multiple dock, panel, and login-polish issues without reopening broad gameplay tuning.

timeline.title: 1.3.0 Fidelity And Trust
timeline.summary: 1.3.0 is now the live Platinum production family, bundling Aurora fidelity and trust work with the first public Galaxy Guardians second-cabinet preview and the first layered platform/application version contract.

timeline.title: 1.3.0.1 Hosted Dev Review
timeline.summary: Hosted /dev carried the post-production conformance review bundle: refreshed dashboards and public docs, conformance economics, application artifact scoring, runtime sprite capture scoring, and a measured calibrated ship-loss audio cue lift. That bundle was reviewed, accepted, and used as the authority-gated public refresh source.

timeline.title: 1.3.0 Production Conformance Refresh
timeline.summary: The accepted 1.3.0.1 hosted-dev review bundle was promoted through hosted `/beta` into hosted `/production`, refreshing the public 1.3.0 family with current conformance docs, provenance, economics, application artifact scoring, runtime sprite scoring, and the measured ship-loss audio lift.

timeline.title: 1.4.0 Multi-Game Public Release
timeline.summary: Hosted `/production` now carries the first deliberate 1.4.0 public family, including the merged Galaxy Guardians playable/conformance tranche, side-by-side conformance entry points, player-two and watch-mode review work, replay/trophy surface polish, and the next stronger multi-game release story for Platinum.

timeline.title: 1.4.1 Patch Candidate
timeline.summary: Hosted `/dev` and hosted `/beta` now name the accumulated follow-through as a real 1.4.1 patch candidate, covering accepted Stage 3 challenge-stage keepers, beta sign-in repair, audio/theme lane clarity, security review refreshes, and release-preflight hardening while hosted `/production` stays on 1.4.0.

timeline.title: Post-1.4.0 Second Application Proof
timeline.summary: Continue the Galaxians-style sibling work after 1.4.0 so Galaxy Guardians can move from second-cabinet proof toward a more durable standalone product promise.

legend.label: Shipped Milestone
legend.detail: A meaningful product step that is already live on hosted `/production`.

legend.label: Release Discipline
legend.detail: Docs, hosted lanes, automation, and release tooling that keep the public surface trustworthy.

legend.label: Trust Fixes
legend.detail: Small player-visible correctness and clarity fixes that belong in a focused patch line.

legend.label: Runtime Hardening
legend.detail: Stability and diagnostics work that keeps late-run failures visible, exportable, and safer before they ever reach public production.

legend.label: Platform Boundary
legend.detail: Work that strengthens the separation between Platinum and the applications it hosts.

legend.label: Second-Application Proof
legend.detail: The smallest useful gameplay slice that proves Platinum can host a meaningfully different game.
